---

## Why It Works

It resonates deeply because I know my audience well. So many of them procrastinate when they should be taking action. I combine that insight with a powerful phrase like "destroy a business…" and it hits well. Then I leave them with a cliffhanger — Too many entrepreneurs: *(click to see more)*

## Template

[Core Problem] can destroy [target audience's goal/objective].
Too many [target audience members]:
- [Common mistake 1]
- [Common mistake 2]
- [Common mistake 3]
Here's what I've learned [source of authority/credibility]:
- [Key lesson 1]
- [Key lesson 2]
- [Key lesson 3]
The most [important adjective] [mistake/lesson/insight]?
[Powerful statement about consequences]!
Remember:
You can't [ineffective approach] your way to [desired outcome].
You have to [effective approach] your way there.

## Example

Perfectionism can destroy leadership potential.
Too many emerging leaders:
- Obsess over every decision
- Fear making any mistakes
- Hide behind preparation
Here's what I've learned coaching 500+ executives:
- Growth requires discomfort
- Leadership is learned through practice
- Impact matters more than intention
The most damaging barrier?
The opportunities for growth you avoid through fear!
Remember:
You can't study your way to leadership.
You have to practice your way there.
